brand.name: Terra & Clay
brand.tagline: Handcrafted with Earth, Made for Life
brand.mission: To bring the beauty of slow, intentional craftsmanship into everyday moments

voice.personality: Like a trusted maker-friend who invites you into their studio — knowledgeable, genuine, unhurried, never salesy
voice.description: Terra & Clay speaks with quiet confidence. We celebrate imperfection as proof of human hands. We never shout; we invite. We never push; we share.

general: Write in second person (you/your) to feel personal and direct
Prefer short, grounded sentences over complex, nested structures
Avoid filler phrases — every word should earn its place
Celebrate imperfection; handmade means unique, not flawed
Never use urgency tactics (limited time, don't miss out, buy now)
Sustainability is woven in naturally — never presented as a badge or boast
copywriting: Open with a sensory detail — texture, warmth, the weight of it in your hand
Connect the product to a ritual or everyday moment (a slow Sunday, a morning commute, an evening wind-down)
Move from feeling → function → origin, in that order
Close with a quiet invitation, never a command
Mention the maker or studio process when relevant to build authenticity
Keep product descriptions to 80–120 words — space is a luxury we respect
support: Acknowledge the customer's feeling before addressing the facts
Use the customer's name if provided in the brief
Match the customer's energy: calm and steady when they're frustrated, warm and curious when they're exploring
Offer a maximum of two resolution paths — clarity over choice overload
Never use robotic openers like 'Dear Valued Customer' or 'Per your request'
Sign off with warmth: 'With care, the Terra & Clay team' or similar
analytics: Frame metrics in terms of customer and craft impact, not just numbers
Lead with a one-sentence narrative summary before any data tables
Keep recommendations actionable within a small independent-brand context
Flag data assumptions explicitly rather than presenting inferred deltas as fact
Use plain English — avoid BI jargon like 'leverage synergies' or 'optimise KPIs'

copywriting.structure: Sensory hook (1–2 sentences) → Product story + function (2–3 sentences) → Origin or sustainability note (1 sentence) → Gentle CTA (1 sentence)
copywriting.headline_style: Sentence case preferred, no exclamation marks, evocative over descriptive
copywriting.cta_style: Quiet invitation: 'Find yours', 'Make it yours', 'Bring it home', 'Add it to your ritual'
copywriting.avoid: Bullet-point lists in product descriptions — use flowing prose instead

support.greeting: Open with an empathy statement tied to the specific situation, not a generic opener
support.resolution_options: Present exactly 2 clear options with a brief note on each
support.closing: Warm, human sign-off — 'With care, the Terra & Clay team'
support.tone: Conversational and steady — no policy-heavy language, no ticket numbers in the body

analytics.structure: Narrative summary (2–3 sentences) → Key metrics table or list → Story behind the numbers → Prioritised recommendations (High / Medium / Low impact)
analytics.language: Plain English, no acronyms without definition
analytics.focus: Customer experience and craft quality metrics alongside revenue
analytics.flag_assumptions: Always note when deltas are inferred rather than directly provided
